opp.name: PremiStar_P2P_June '26
opp.ais_why:
• PremiStar is at Qualified on a P2P motion with Merlin and Merlin Intake scoped and no discovery description recorded.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and no pain points or AI-requirement are captured.
• Evidence is limited to product scope, insufficient to place AI appetite above exploratory.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or value case exists in any source. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c are unpopulated. No Avoma calls have been conducted to capture the buyer's articulated pain, cost impact, headcount reduction targets, cycle time improvement goals, or ROI expectations. The product scope (iSave, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) suggests procurement transformation and AI-driven intake automation as potential value drivers, but no buyer has confirmed this. Without a quantified pain statement the deal cannot progress beyond early qualification. The immediate move is to conduct a discovery call that extracts the specific operational or financial pain this buyer is trying to solve, ideally with numbers attached (e.g., manual invoice error rate, supplier negotiation cycle time, procurement headcount cost).

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified in any source. Salesforce field X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is unpopulated. No contact roles exist in Salesforce, so no one is marked as Champion. No Avoma calls have occurred to reveal an internal advocate who has access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, who is actively selling Zycus internally, or who has shared competitive intelligence or the buyer's internal timeline. For a complex procurement platform sale a champion is essential to navigate the buying committee, coach the deal team on internal politics, and drive urgency. The absence of a champion leaves the deal vulnerable to stalling, dark periods, or competitor infiltration. The move is to identify a likely champion candidate (often a Procurement Manager, Business Analyst, or project lead who is bought into the solution) and deliberately invest in building that relationship through value-adds, exclusive insights, and access to Zycus executives or product teams.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source. Salesforce field Competitors__c is unpopulated. Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, and Why_not_Zycus__c are all unpopulated. No Avoma calls have been conducted to surface whether the buyer is benchmarking against Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP, or another procurement platform, or whether they are running a sole-source evaluation. The absence of competitive intelligence means Zycus cannot tailor its differentiation narrative, cannot position Merlin as a unique AI wedge, and cannot anticipate objections or pricing pressure. For a deal this early the buyer may not have revealed competitors yet, or this may be a sole-sourced partner-led opportunity. The move is to ask the buyer directly on the first discovery call whether they are evaluating other vendors, what their current procurement stack looks like, and what gaps in that stack are driving this evaluation. If sole-sourced, confirm why and secure a commitment to an exclusive timeline.

identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by a buyer in any source. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c are unpopulated. No Avoma calls have been conducted to capture the buyer's current-state struggles. The product scope (iSave for supplier negotiation, eProcurement orchestration, eInvoicing automation, Merlin Intake for AI-driven requisition handling) implies potential pain points such as manual procurement workflows, slow invoice processing, limited supplier negotiation leverage, and lack of AI-powered intake automation, but no buyer has confirmed any of this. Without a named pain owner and a confirmed pain statement the deal lacks urgency and a compelling event. The move is to conduct a discovery call that identifies who owns the pain (VP Procurement, CFO, Operations lead) and extracts their articulated struggle with the current state.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting or paper process has been documented in any source. No Salesforce fields capture MSA/SOW mechanics, legal review requirements, procurement approval workflows, or InfoSec/compliance reviews. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is unpopulated so no forcing function from an expiring incumbent contract is visible. No Avoma calls have occurred to discuss redlining expectations, preferred contract templates, or partner/SI involvement in implementation scoping. For a $100k SaaS + services deal the paper process often includes InfoSec questionnaires, data privacy addendums, ERP integration scoping, and multi-party MSA negotiation, but none of this has been surfaced. The move is to ask the buyer about their standard contracting workflow, legal approval chain, and any InfoSec or compliance gates that could extend the timeline.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified in any source. Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c are all unpopulated. No contact roles exist in Salesforce. No Avoma calls have occurred to surface who controls budget authority or commercial negotiation. For a $100k procurement transformation deal the EB is typically a VP of Procurement, CFO, or Head of Supply Chain, but no such person has been mapped. Without EB identification the deal cannot move to commercials or proposal stages. The move is to ask the initial contact (if one exists) who will approve the budget and sign the contract, then secure a call with that person to validate the commercial timeline and authority.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process has been mapped from any source. Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c and Mandate__c are unpopulated. Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Multiple_approvals__c are not set. No contact roles exist to infer a buying committee structure. No Avoma calls have occurred to reveal the approval chain, milestone sequence (RFI, RFP, shortlist, demos, commercials, legal review), or the roles involved at each gate. For a procurement platform deal the process typically involves Procurement, IT, Finance, Legal, and InfoSec, but none of this has been confirmed. Without a process map the deal timeline and close date are speculative. The move is to ask the buyer to walk through their vendor selection steps and approval gates, naming who participates at each stage and what deliverables are required to advance.

decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria have been documented in any source. Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are unpopulated. No Avoma calls have been conducted to capture the buyer's evaluation framework, must-have vs nice-to-have features, or scoring model. The product scope (Merlin Intake alongside iSave, eProcurement, eInvoicing) implies the buyer may value AI-driven automation and procurement orchestration, but no buyer has articulated how they will compare vendors or what makes a solution acceptable. Without criteria Zycus cannot tailor demos, positioning, or proposals. The move is to run a structured discovery or scoping session that extracts the buyer's evaluation scorecard, technical requirements, and deal-breakers, ideally documented in a BRD or RFP response.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 4 reflects a weak position: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum is flat at 50: no forward or backward motion was observed.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (4), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

items: Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-30 (2 days ago); deal is brand new.
Zero Avoma calls discovered for opportunity or account; no buyer conversations recorded.
No contact roles in Salesforce; stakeholder map is empty.
No MEDDPICC coverage across all elements.
Products scope is defined (iSave, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake) but not validated with a buyer.
confidence_signals.summary: Deal is 2 days old with last Salesforce activity 2026-06-30. Engagement is nascent but not yet cooling. Zero Avoma calls and zero contact roles create high uncertainty about buyer intent, urgency, and qualification depth.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 424. Time in Qualified: 2 days (created 2026-06-30, swept 2026-07-02). No historical stage progression. No forward slip. Required pace: approximately 28 days per major milestone across 15 stages to reach close by 2027-08-30 is achievable, but zero buyer touch, zero calls, and no MEDDPICC coverage puts execution at immediate risk despite the long runway.
evidence: Deal created 2026-06-30, last Salesforce activity 2026-06-30 (2 days ago)
Zero Avoma calls discovered across opportunity and account
No MEDDPICC coverage: no EB, DM, champion, pain articulation, or decision criteria
Products scope defined: iSave, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, Merlin Intake
Pipeline forecast appropriate given absence of validation
